summary: Comprehensive testing of new features: demo data seeding (Reviews/Social), image upload endpoint, Create Post image upload UI, and OAuth redirect URI dynamic verification. All 19 backend tests and all frontend UI tests passed. The app is fully functional.

minor.issue: API returns 403 (Forbidden) instead of 401 (Unauthorized) when no auth token provided. Standard HTTP convention says 401 for missing credentials, 403 for insufficient permissions. This is a minor semantic issue.

issues: Uses native browser date/time pickers (input type=date/time) for schedule functionality. Not using shadcn Calendar component. This is functional but may look inconsistent on some browsers.

critical_code_review_comments: API consistently returns 403 instead of 401 for unauthenticated requests - check auth middleware (get_current_user / require_role). FastAPI's OAuth2PasswordBearer should return 401 by default. This may affect client-side handling of auth errors.

Frontend api/index.js interceptor only redirects to /login on 401 status - since API returns 403 for auth errors, this means the auto-redirect-on-auth-failure interceptor would NOT trigger on expired/missing tokens, potentially leaving users stuck.
seed_data_creation: Demo data was seeded and cleared as part of testing. Reviews (6 added/cleared) and Social Posts (4 added/cleared) via /api/seed/demo-data endpoint. Pages left clean after testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All features from this iteration work correctly: (1) Reviews page Load Demo Data / Clear Demo / display with platform icons, star ratings, status badges, (2) Social page Load Demo Data / Clear Demo / post display / comment expansion, (3) CreatePost image upload area visible below textarea, Publish Now/Schedule tabs working, (4) Platforms page shows dynamic Redirect URI using window.location.origin. AWS S3 upload falls back to local storage (credentials not set) - tested /api/uploads/images with a minimal JPEG successfully stores locally. Minor observation: API returns 403 not 401 for unauthenticated requests - this could break the frontend auth interceptor which only checks for status 401.

rca_of_issue.403_vs_401: The backend's require_role() and get_current_user() functions in auth.py likely raise HTTPException(status_code=403) instead of 401. Check auth.py's get_current_user function - it should raise 401 for missing/invalid credentials. The frontend api/index.js interceptor only handles 401 for auto-logout, meaning expired or missing tokens on 403 response won't trigger auto-redirect to login. Root cause: auth.py should use status_code=401 for authentication failures (missing/invalid token), and 403 only for authorization failures (valid token but insufficient role).
